sections.title: STANDARD FOR CAPE GOOSEBERRY
sections.text:
Adopted in 2001. Amended in 2005, 2011.

Commonly known in certain regions by: physalis, capuli, groseilles du Cap, amour en cage, baguenaude, Lanterne japonaise, etc.
sections.retrieval_text:
STANDARD FOR CAPE GOOSEBERRY
level=1
Adopted in 2001. Amended in 2005, 2011.

Commonly known in certain regions by: physalis, capuli, groseilles du Cap, amour en cage, baguenaude, Lanterne japonaise, etc.

page_spans.text:
Adopted in 2001. Amended in 2005, 2011.

Commonly known in certain regions by: physalis, capuli, groseilles du Cap, amour en cage, baguenaude, Lanterne japonaise, etc.

sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of cape gooseberries grown from Physalis peruviana (L.), of the Solanaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Cape gooseberries for industrial processing are excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to commercial varieties of cape gooseberries grown from Physalis peruviana (L.), of the Solanaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Cape gooseberries for industrial processing are excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of cape gooseberries grown from Physalis peruviana (L.), of the Solanaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Cape gooseberries for industrial processing are excluded.

sections.title: The cape gooseberries must have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness account being taken of the characteristics of the variety and the area in which they are grown.
path_titles: The cape gooseberries must have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness account being taken of the characteristics of the variety and the area in which they are grown.
sections.text:
The development and condition of the cape gooseberries must be such as to enable them: 
­ to withstand transport and handling; and 
­ to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

sections.title: Maturity Requirements
sections.text: The maturity of the cape gooseberry can be visually assessed from its external colouring, which changes from green to orange as the fruit ripens. Its condition can be confirmed by determining total soluble solids. A change in colouring of the calyx is not indicative of ripening of the fruit.  The soluble solids content should be at least 14.0°Brix.
sections.retrieval_text:

level=3

The soluble solids content should be at least 14.0°Brix.

page_spans.text: The maturity of the cape gooseberry can be visually assessed from its external colouring, which changes from green to orange as the fruit ripens. Its condition can be confirmed by determining total soluble solids. A change in colouring of the calyx is not indicative of ripening of the fruit.  The soluble solids content should be at least 14.0°Brix.

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text: Cape gooseberries in this class must be of superior quality.  They must be characteristic of the variety and/or  commercial type. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text:
Size is determined by the maximum diameter of the equatorial section of the fruit, with a minimum diameter of 15 mm, in accordance with the following table:
Size Code Diameter (mm)
A 15.0 – 18.0
B 18.1 – 20.0
C 20.1 – 22.0
D > 22.1

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

sections.title: Size Tolerances
sections.text: For all classes, 10% by number or weight of cape gooseberries corresponding to the size immediately above and/or below that indicated on the package.

sections.title: Class I
sections.retrieval_text:
2 > 2.2 > Class I
number=2.2.2
level=3
Cape gooseberries in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package:
- slight defects in shape;
- slight defects in colouring;
- slight skin defects.
The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit.

sections.title: Class II
“Extra” Class
sections.text: Five percent by number or weight of cape gooseberries with or without calyx not satisfying the requirements of the class, but meeting those of Class I or, exceptionally, coming within the tolerances of that class.

sections.title: Class I
sections.text: Ten percent by number or weight of cape gooseberries with or without calyx not satisfying the requirements of the class, but meeting those of Class II or, exceptionally, coming within the tolerances of that class.

sections.title: Class II
sections.text: Ten percent by number or weight of cape gooseberries with or without calyx satisfying neither the requirements of the class nor the minimum requirements, with the exception of produce affected by severe bruising, rotting or any other deterioration rendering it unfit for consumption. Up to a maximum of 20% by number or weight of fruit with small healed cracks covering an area greater than 5% is accepted in this class.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).
sections.retrieval_text:
CONTAMINANTS
number=7
level=1

page_spans.text: The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).

sections.title: Uniformity
sections.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only cape gooseberries of the same origin, variety, quality, colouring, size and type of presentation (with or without calyx). The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

level=2

page_spans.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only cape gooseberries of the same origin, variety, quality, colouring, size and type of presentation (with or without calyx). The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.title: Packaging
sections.text: Cape gooseberries must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new 3, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Cape gooseberries shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 44-1995).

sections.title: Description of Containers
sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the cape gooseberries. Packages must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: 8.1 It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice. 8.2 The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

pages.title: STANDARD FOR CAPE GOOSEBERRY
pages.summary: Página apresenta o título principal do documento em caixa alta sem numeração, atuando como nível 1. Identificado também o cabeçalho repetitivo 'CXS 226-2001'. Texto associado ao título principal descreve informações de adoção e nomes regionais, sem subdivisões estruturais.

Esta página contém os títulos principais numerados 1 e 2 como nível 1, com o título 2 subdividido em níveis 2 e 3, refletindo a estrutura hierárquica dos critérios e classificações. O texto foi associado de forma precisa a cada nó, respeitando a continuidade e os critérios de título numérico. Não há indicação de continuidade entre páginas nesta página.

Esta página contém seções detalhadas dos níveis 1 a 3, iniciando com os títulos numéricos 2.2.2 e 2.2.3 como nível 3, seguidos dos grandes títulos em caixa alta dos níveis 1 e 2 para as seções 3 e 4 e suas subseções numeradas, com texto associado corretamente e continuidade conservada da página anterior para algumas seções.

A página contém três grandes seções de nível 1 numeradas (5, 6, 7) com seus respectivos subtítulos hierarquizados até nível 3, com texto associado claramente delimitado. A estrutura hierárquica está consistente, todos os títulos aparecem na página e não há indicações de continuidade para outras páginas. O cabeçalho da página é identificado, e nenhuma nova identificação do título do documento foi detectada.

A página contém a seção principal '8. HYGIENE' com dois subtópicos numerados (8.1 e 8.2) incorporados no texto da seção principal. A seção '8' está em continuidade a partir da página anterior, pois o título aparece mas o início da seção já vem da página anterior (segmento anterior: 7.2). Não há continuidade para a próxima página. O cabeçalho da página foi identificado. Document title não detectado nesta página.
